summary: The review of the collaboration agreement indicates a generally low-risk posture, with most flagged clauses falling outside the scope of the University of Auckland's Preferred Contracting Positions. However, several amber and red flags highlight critical issues requiring attention. Key concerns include intellectual property clauses that must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd, indemnity provisions that deviate from UoA's liability limitations, and termination rights that conflict with UoA's preferred positions. Additionally, publication and confidentiality clauses require adjustments to align with UoA's policies on academic freedom and disclosure rights. The next step is to escalate the flagged clauses to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, and Auckland UniServices Ltd for review and amendment to ensure compliance with UoA's contracting standards.

flags.clause_title: 6 INDEMNITY
flags.snippet: 6 INDEMNITY
flags.rationale: The clause text '6 INDEMNITY' is insufficiently detailed to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-08. Indemnities are a sensitive topic, and the clause must explicitly address key requirements: (1) indemnities offered only for damage/injury caused by UoA's negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct, (2) liability under indemnity must be within the Liability Limit, (3) proportionate reduction for contributory negligence and obligations to mitigate losses must be included, and (4) indemnities against IP infringement must be excluded unless specific exceptions apply (e.g., Trusted Funder through contestable funding award). The lack of detail triggers escalation to the Research Contract Delegations for review and amendment.
flags.text: 6 INDEMNITY

flags.clause_title: 6.2 Each party (“Indemnifying Party”) indemnifies and agrees to keep
flags.snippet: 6.2 Each party (Indemnifying Party) indemnifies and agrees to keep indemnified the other parties, their respective directors, officers, employees and agents (those Indemnified) from and against any claim, action, da
flags.rationale: This clause materially deviates from UoA Position #POS-07 on indemnities and liability limitations. It offers broad indemnities without limiting liability to direct losses or capping exposure to NZD$500,000, as required by UoA's preferred position. Additionally, it includes indemnification for IP infringement, which is explicitly excluded under UoA's preferred and acceptable positions unless the project is funded by a Trusted Funder through a contestable funding award. The clause also fails to include proportionate reduction for contributory negligence or obligations to mitigate losses. Escalation to Research Grants and Contracts Delegations and Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) is required.
flags.text:
6.2 Each party (“Indemnifying Party”) indemnifies and agrees to keep indemnified the other parties, their respective directors, officers, employees and agents (“those Indemnified”) from and against any claim, action, damage, loss, liability, cost, charge, expense or payment however incurred or suffered by those Indemnified as a result of:  
a) any breach of the Grant Agreement, negligence or misfeasance occasioned by that the Indemnifying Party;  
b) an unlawful or negligent act or omission by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors in connection with performance of this Agreement; and  
c) any actual or alleged infringement of a Party’s or a third party’s IP rights by the Indemnifying Party or any of its directors, officers, employees, agents or contractors.

flags.clause_title: 7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties
flags.snippet: 7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties agree that [REDACTED] may make Project-related publications at any time.
flags.rationale: The clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication. While it allows publication, it does not preserve the Principal Investigator's explicit right to publish, nor does it address reasonable patent stand-down periods or student publication rights. The absence of these protections materially deviates from the Preferred tier. Escalation to Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required to ensure compliance with UoA's publication policy.
flags.text: 7.1 Subject to the confidentiality restrictions in clause 8, the Parties agree that [REDACTED] may make Project-related publications at any time.

flags.snippet: 7.2 The Participating Organisations may publish material relating to the Project, provided all material sought to be published is submitted for approval and approved by [REDACTED] at least thirty (30) days prior to the 
flags.rationale: The clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication. UoA's preferred position grants the Principal Investigator the right to publish, with a reasonable patent stand-down of no more than 60 days. Students retain publication rights for theses or academic publications, including commercially sensitive content. While a stand-down period of 30 days is within acceptable limits, the requirement for approval by [REDACTED] introduces a restriction that is not aligned with UoA's preferred or acceptable positions. Additionally, the clause allows [REDACTED] to amend the proposed publication to remove Confidential Information, which could further restrict academic freedom. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.
flags.text: 7.2 The Participating Organisations may publish material relating to the Project, provided all material sought to be published is submitted for approval and approved by [REDACTED] at least thirty (30) days prior to the intended publication (“review period”). Approval will not be unreasonably withheld nor delayed. [REDACTED] will treat the proposed publication in confidence and may during the review period amend the proposed publication to remove any Confidential Information of [REDACTED].

flags.clause_title: 9 TERMINATION
flags.snippet: 9 TERMINATION
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on Termination. Specifically, it lacks clarity on mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, UoA's right to terminate for non-payment, and provisions for termination costs including uncancellable obligations. This omission triggers escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential amendment.
flags.text: 9 TERMINATION

flags.clause_title: 9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least
flags.snippet: 9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least 30 days notice in writing to the other Parties.
flags.rationale: The clause allows termination without cause, which is outside the UoA Preferred Position for Termination (POS-19). Termination for convenience is only acceptable for Consulting contracts, with reasonable notice, PI agreement, and the client paying termination costs including uncancellable obligations. This clause does not specify these conditions, nor does it limit termination for convenience to Consulting contracts. It materially deviates from the Preferred Position and triggers escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.
flags.text: 9.2 [Redacted] may terminate this Agreement without cause by giving at least 30 days notice in writing to the other Parties.

flags.clause_title: 11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION
flags.snippet: 11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide any substantive detail about the dispute resolution process, making it impossible to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-18. The Preferred tier requires good faith discussions, escalation to senior management, mediation before arbitration/litigation, proceedings in English, and allowance for urgent interlocutory court action. The absence of these elements triggers escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts or General Counsel for review.
flags.text: 11 DISPUTE RESOLUTION

flags.clause_title: 12.12  A Participating Organisation must not subcontract the performance of
flags.snippet: 12.12 A Participating Organisation must not subcontract the performance of all or any part of that Participating Organisation’s obligations under this Agreement without the prior consent of [Redacted] which shall not be unreasonably withheld. Each Participating Organisation is responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of its subcontractors as if they were those of the Party itself.
flags.rationale: The clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-07 on Liability Limitations and Exclusions. The clause imposes unlimited liability on the Participating Organisation for all acts and omissions of its subcontractors, which is inconsistent with UoA's preferred position that liability must be capped to the lesser of NZD$500,000 or the contract value, and limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct. Additionally, the clause does not exclude consequential or indirect losses, nor does it align with acceptable carve-outs for liability caps. Escalation to Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is required.
